key_insight: LuckyStar 111B shows that a fully post-trained multilingual model can be efficiently adapted into a Korean-English tool-using agent using a small set of targeted training and serving tricks.
engineering_relevance: The paper offers a practical blueprint for enterprise deployment under memory constraints by combining preamble-based mode switching, verifiable RL, language-consistency rewards, and 4-bit quantization.
method: Start from Cohere Command A instead of pretraining from scratch.
Use multilingual supervised fine-tuning plus reinforcement learning on verifiable multi-step tool-use tasks.
Add language-consistency rewards for Korean responses and 4-bit quantization for single-GPU serving.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports improvements in mathematical reasoning, function calling, and agentic NL2SQL while maintaining general Korean and English instruction-following quality.
watch_item: Quantization and reward shaping may preserve performance only under the specific enterprise workflows and language settings tested, so broader generalization and failure modes still need careful validation.
paper_title: Think in English, Answer in Korean: Efficient Adaptation of Multilingual Tool-Using Agents
